experiences.title: Founder
experiences.jobDescription:
College Match LLC is now a part of Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub.
Our vision: Transforming College Admissions with an AI-powered platform to simplify the college application process. We help high school students discover ideal colleges, build personalized lists, and navigate admissions effortlessly. Join us on the path to higher education success.

experiences.title: Senior Software Development Engineer
Senior Software Development Engineer
Software Development Engineer
experiences.jobDescription: As a software development engineer, I’m responsible for designing, developing, maintaining and troubleshooting Continuous Deployment service and website. The primary work involves pipeline features such as workflow, package building, version set filtering and publishing, environment promotion, etc. The primary skill stack covers Java/J2EE, Ruby on Rails/Capybara and Rspec, Spring (DI and AOP), Hibernate ORM/JPA, HTML/CSS, JQuery/Javascript/Ajax, MySQL, data modeling, Maven/Ant, Git, multi-threading programming, Lucene/Compass.

experiences.title: Research and Teaching Assistant
experiences.jobDescription: Research Areas: IP lookup, Packet Classification, Hash Tables, SDN and OpenFlow.

experiences.title: Program architect and chief developer
experiences.jobDescription: As the program architect and chief developer, led a 4-member group to develop a telecommunication platform to provide text and multimedia message service for a large number of cellphone users. The platform runs stably and gains credits for excellent availability, scalability, security owing to modular design, high-quality code and hierarchical access control.

updates.postText: Welcome to College Insight! We're your go-to resource for simplifying the college application journey. Our intelligent tools help you build a tailored college list, from dream schools to safeties. Explore our Roadmap and Academy, covering a wide range of topics. Plus, enjoy a FREE 6-month trial at https://collegeinsight.ai. Subscribe and stay tuned for more helpful content!

updates.postText:
What is a balanced college list to include your safety, target and dream schools
The college application process has numerous components, making it challenging for students and their families to determine how or where to begin. However, creating a well-rounded college list is a crucial and essential aspect of the process. Starting in junior year provides ample time for researching, campus visits, and eliminating schools that do not meet your criteria.

publications.title: A highly deterministic hashing scheme using bitmap filter for high speed networking
publications.summary: By means of novel collision resolution mechanisms high deterministic performance is achieved in our scheme. An important category of false positive is reduced to 0 and unnecessary computation for filter query is avoided. Through mathematical reasoning we propose a hierarchy with optimal load in each hash table and low discard rate. Simulation results adequately justify the efficiency of re-balancing and incremental update. The comparison to other schemes distinguishes effective use of the exact-summary, bitmap filter in our scheme. In conclusion our scheme is highly qualified for hardware implementation to boost network processing. Future work intends to focus on rehashing to accommodate more frequent updates and multiple next hops in a BH table bucket.

publications.title: OpenFlow Accelerator: a Decomposition-based Hashing Approach for Flow Processing
publications.summary:
To support scalable, flexible software-defined networking, OpenFlow is designed to provide granular traffic control across multiple vendor’s network devices for efficient flow processing. Decision-tree packet classification algorithms do not scale to the number of flow table fields while decomposition algorithms such as RFC fail to provide necessary incremental update and determinism. 
  Since searching in a single field is well studied, e.g. Longest Prefix Match (LPM) for prefix fields, we propose a decomposition approach which performs individual search on each flow table field, aggregates these results and conducts a query in a single hash table. Our approach scales well to the number of fields and allows incremental update. Meanwhile deterministic query is enabled for high-speed search. As far as we know our proposal is the first efficient decomposition approach to address multidimensional match in an OpenFlow flow table with an arbitrary number of fields as well as any match type. Theoretical analysis and experiments using synthetic classifiers justify the
performance improvement.

publications.title: A Hierarchical Hashing Scheme to Accelerate Longest Prefix Matching
publications.summary:
We propose a hierarchical hashing scheme to handle LPM for IP lookup. We survey real routing tables and make two crucial observations upon the distribution of prefix lengths. Based on the observations we combine bitmap and hashing mechanisms to build up a two-layer data structure to efficiently contain and encode prefixes. Through theoretical analysis and implementation evaluation on real IPv4/IPv6 routing tables, our scheme outperforms TBMP and BSOL in every performance
metric. We can improve current work by saving memory consumption and using better collision resolution policy. As we indicate in section V, use of any bit compression skill definitely reduces memory consumption of bitmap in Bnode with extremely low APD values. Use of better collision  resolution can further reduce the average length of Bnode list when hash tables have high load factor.

publications.title: TCAM-based classification using divide and-conquer for range expansion
publications.summary:
As the first scheme using divide-and-conquer to address range expansion problem which sharply degrades storage performance for TCAM-based packet classification approaches, our proposal presents the first practical application architecture for hybrid utilization of various schemes for  further TCAM storage improvement. According to our scheme any range is either optimally represented by exactly one TCAM entry, or expressed using another best suitable scheme. Therefore TCAM storage performance is further improved. Under DCS application architecture, TCAM entries can be flexibly divided into blocks upon which searching in constant time can be
performed in parallel. Simplicity of block division and encoding methods using available unused bits in TCAM entries as well as trivial computation overhead in searching lead to ease of practical implementation and deployment of our scheme without hardware modification. Theoretical analysis
and simulation using synthetic packet classifiers justify our architecture’s excellent performance in fundamental metrics such as storage efficiency, preprocessing, update, and scalability.

publications.title: Protecting Run-time Filters for Network Intrusion Detection Systems
publications.source: in Proceedings of the International Conference on Advanced Information Networking and Applications (AINA)
publications.summary: Run-time filters are used by many Network Intrusion Detection Systems (NIDS) to reduce load and make Deep Packet Inspection feasible.  However, attackers can easily craft traffic that can bypass these run-time filters.  We provide a method for safeguarding filters by imposing rate-limiting as well as providing a parallel anomaly detector and audit trail to bolster the NIDS.

recommendationsReceived.name: Jikai (Maggie) Zhu
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Software Engineer at Microsoft
recommendationsReceived.context: Jikai (Maggie) worked with Hai on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: It's been an honor working with Harry as he is not only a great SDE on coding but also about problem solving and design. As a Senior Developer, he was able to take a lot into consideration and he really care about his code quality. Harry is also always helpful and supportive for his coworkers. We know he can be depended on when anything happens. Any employee would be lucky to have Harry.

recommendationsReceived.name: Alan Cheslow
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Retired from 30+ years in tech
recommendationsReceived.context: Alan worked with Hai but on different teams
recommendationsReceived.description: At UIEvolution Harry has been an absolutely vital member of a scrum team that is delivering a variety of complex cloud services for one of our largest customer projects. His software engineering knowledge and expertise is top-caliber, and his deliverables consistently meet or exceed all of my expectations. Harry is proactive, collaborative, and a true pleasure to work with and depend on. He will make a great addition to any team lucky enough to have him.

recommendations.name: Jill D.
recommendations.subtitle: Owner | Woofie’s of Scottsdale & Paradise Valley
recommendations.context: Hai worked with Jill on the same team
recommendations.description:
I’ve had the pleasure of working with Jill for five years at Microsoft on the same Azure Cloud+AI team (Lead Scoring), where we focus on machine learning solutions with highly scalable and reliable production capabilities to support digital marketing goals. Jill is an exceptional project manager who consistently demonstrates leadership, strategic thinking, and a results-driven approach, guiding our multi-million-dollar projects with clarity and precision.

Her ability to manage stakeholders and proactively address challenges ensures that our projects stay on track and deliver impactful results. Thanks to her meticulous planning and execution, we’ve consistently achieved success and driven significant revenue growth.

Jill’s dedication, collaborative spirit, and commitment to excellence make her an invaluable asset to our team. I highly recommend her for any future endeavors.

recommendations.name: Prabhat Jana
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Technology Architect at Infosys | Microsoft Azure | C# .NET Core | Azure Machine Learning
recommendations.context: Hai worked with Prabhat on the same team
recommendations.description:
Prabhat has been an integral part of the Lead Scoring engineering team, making outstanding contributions across security enhancements, application development, compliance, CI/CD pipelines, and more. What truly impresses me is his exceptional work in delivering our machine learning model scoring framework using Azure Machine Learning Service online endpoints.

As a key ML DevOps engineer, he has played a crucial role in implementing core components of our Web API and AMLS backend, skillfully balancing performance, scalability, security, geographic distribution, and reliability. His expertise and dedication have been invaluable to our team, and I deeply appreciate his contributions.

recommendations.name: Jikai (Maggie) Zhu
recommendations.subtitle: Software Engineer at Microsoft
recommendations.context: Hai worked with Jikai (Maggie) on the same team
recommendations.description: Maggie is a wonderful software engineer when I work with her at Globys. We contributed to several projects together and she always impressed me with good quality of code and stable delivery. Whenever any scrum ticket is handed out to her, I definitely trust her capability to get it resolved in a timely, satisfactory manner because she's very good at coping with a wide variety of problems from Core development to Client delivery. Our project benefits from her great effort in every SDLC aspect: design, coding, testing, building and deployment. Besides she's an excellent team player when communicating with any team role: Dev, Ops, Product Manager, Project Manager, QA, Solution Architect and clients. As a full-stack Dev, she's also proficient in intensive knowledge categories: from SQL to distributed caching, from .Net to React, from Jenkins building to Linux bash scripting, and so on.

recommendations.name: Jeff Herold
recommendations.subtitle: Director - Technical Program Manager
recommendations.context: Hai worked with Jeff on the same team
recommendations.description: Jeff is definitely among one of the best program managers I've been collaborated! As an awesome leader in our Business Intelligence project, Jeff takes great care of client requirement, project progress and product delivery. Without his strong support I cannot imagine where our BI project will go. I'm deeply impressed by his commitment and ownership from the very beginning.

summary: Hai Sun is currently working at College Match LLC and is building College Insight AI, an early-stage edtech product aimed at making U.S. college planning smarter, more affordable, and accessible to every high school student and family. Their posts emphasize mission-driven product development—positioning an AI platform as a low-cost alternative to expensive college consultants—and they actively solicit collaborators to help scale outreach to students and parents. Hai's tone is approachable, collaborative, and candid: they mix product and growth calls-to-action with personal appeals (including an #OpenToWork post), using conversational language and occasional emojis to invite dialogue. Content themes focus on college admissions, affordability, Gen Z outreach, social and digital growth strategies, and storytelling-driven marketing for education. They tend to post pragmatic, problem-solving messages that aim to connect with practitioners (growth marketers, community builders) and the broader network for support or introductions, while occasionally sharing topical news items. Overall, Hai projects a blend of product leadership, grassroots growth orientation, and a strong commitment to educational access and community-driven solutions.
